description:
Operating Area: Adriatic Sea, Croatia

Sailing along the Dalmatian coast in your private luxury mini cruiser Cataleya with all of today’s modern amenities is an experience you won’t miss. The CATALEYA has been designed by experts who know what passengers need, not just for comfort but also for safety! She can hold up to 21 people comfortably and still provide them that intimate feel while out aboard at any destination around Croatia.

The Cataleya sun deck with mattresses and pillows is a popular area for the sailing time to relax and enjoy views of the Adriatic islands. Take the ship’s benefits and get refreshed in the Jacuzzi before heading back into the open sea!

There is an open dining space on the main deck with a beautiful scenic view. For relief from the heat, head inside to a comfortable lounge equipped with air conditioning and a television, where you and your friends may relax with a meal and a few beverages while watching your favorite show.

Nine airy and well-equipped cabins are perfect for a relaxing getaway. The rooms come equipped with comfortable beds, enough space to store clothes or other items you may need on your vacation, and private bathrooms containing all amenities.

Cataleya has some great water sports equipment, which means you can enjoy paddle boarding and water skiing. It also includes snorkeling equipment to complete your vacation with a day of underwater exploration!

When you’re looking for a private getaway, nothing beats the joy of sharing your vacation with someone who knows what they are doing. This family-owned boat, with a long tradition of yacht charter and a carefully selected crew, will make your travel dreams come true!

crew.name: Marin Trgo
crew.bio:
Born in Split May 1988 where he also finished his high school education for tourism and hotel management, he worked in his family business almost most of his life as a hospitality manager.

As a family they have a strong connection with the sea so 3 years ago they have decided to buy their first boat for nautical tourism. His parents stayed in restaurant business and Marin went to the sea to finally put together his love of the sea and hospitality.

From the beginning he was recognized in the business due to his knowledge and experience; he regularly won the prize for the best service on board but that was not enough for him, massive cabin charter tourism was not what fulfill Marin.

So, he sold his boat and bought beautiful Cataleya and the only thing he is hoping for is that with his experience and enthusiasm he will be able to provide his guests with an unforgettable vacation.

You will love Marin from the first moment you meet him, he is a very outgoing and cheerful person who is always there for his guests. He speaks English and some German and Italian.

crew.name: Karlo Domazet
crew.bio:
Sailor Karlo was born in 1998 in the city of Split, Croatia. However, he spent his formative years growing up in the neigh-boring town of Solin. Coming from a family of entrepreneurs, Karlo was imbued with a strong work ethic and a sense of responsibility from a young age.

Driven by his passion for the sea and maritime exploration, Karlo enrolled in the Naval School in Split, where he embarked on his journey to pursue a career in sailing. Despite the demanding nature of his studies, Karlo’s determination knew no bounds. He understood the value of hard work and responsibility and was unwavering in his commitment to achieving his goals.

To finance his education and support himself through his studies, Karlo took on various jobs, displaying his resilience and resourcefulness. Whether working part-time at local establishments or taking on freelance opportunities, Karlo approached each task with diligence and dedication.

Known for his approachability and willingness to help, Karlo is the kind of person one can always rely on, no matter the circumstances.

He finds joy in the tranquility of the sea, often spending his free time sailing along the breathtaking Croatian coastlines. His love for the ocean and adventurous spirit are evident in his pursuits on and off the water.

crew.name: Nemanja Kolarac
crew.bio:
Nemanja Kolarac was born on October 16, 1983. He pursued his education at the Hospitality and Tourism School, specializing as a culinary technician. After this, he continued his studies at Hospitality College.

Throughout his career, Nemanja garnered extensive experience in various Belgrade restaurants, specializing in international and Italian cuisine. Additionally, he worked in several catering companies, catering to corporate events and concerts for renowned artists such as Madonna, Sting, and the Rolling Stones.

Notable highlights of his career include working at the Intercontinental Hotel and Holiday Inn, where he catered to athletes and teams during the 2009 Summer Universiade in Belgrade, adhering to halal and kosher standards.

Outside of work, Nemanja enjoys cycling, jewelry making, and origami and is keenly interested in history. He is a licensed driver, fluent in English, and adept at handling pressure. Furthermore, he actively engages in philanthropic endeavors, volunteering as a blood donor for over twenty hours and being part of the European bone marrow donor database. He is also a devoted father of three children.

crew.name: Ivan Grčić
crew.bio:
Ivan Grčić was born and raised in Sinj. With a background in tourism and hospitality, Ivan has dedicated his life to guiding others through destinations, ensuring that every moment spent at sea is imbued with wonder and authenticity.

As a deckhand on this sea voyage, Ivan’s presence promises an adventure filled with discoveries, companionship, and unforgettable moments. Each guest under Ivan’s care is invited into a shared and intensely personal story, making each journey across the sea a chapter worth enjoying.

Looking forward to shared experiences, Ivan stands ready at the helm, eager to weave the unique stories of everyone on board into the fabric of this beautiful journey.

crew.name: Luka Erek
crew.bio:
Luka was born in 1995 in a beautiful city by the sea called Šibenik, where he lived for 12 years before moving to Argentina. He stayed there a year, finishing the sixth grade, and moved to Zagreb. He has a BA degree in sociology from the University of Zagreb.

During his student days, Luka started bartending and has been doing it since. From his first job in an alehouse in Zagreb through a cocktail bar in Vodice before embarking on Cataleya, he also has the experience of working in the National Park “Kornati” so he can share his knowledge and explain to the guests why it is such a fantastic place to visit.

Luka speaks English and, thanks to his grandmother, who spent her working career in Germany, German as well.

For Luka, working on Cataleya will be an immense pleasure and honor, as he loves the sea and is very excited to join the crew this summer and serve the guests.
